goal: Promote `search_code` from its WO-0035 / P1-W5-F09 KG-symbol + in-process-ripgrep merge shape to the master-plan §5.2 G2 weighted-RRF fusion shape: ripgrep + Probe (stdio MCP plugin) + LanceDB (per-branch vector store) all run in parallel, fuse via WO-0056's `fuse_g2_rrf` with weights {Probe×2.0, Ripgrep×1.5, Lancedb×1.5} and `k=60`, and surface the fused result on a NEW additive `_meta.g2_fused` field while preserving every legacy `_meta` field of the existing handler verbatim per DEC-0015 D1. This WO refreshes WO-0057 (emitted 2026-05-06T22:30Z, never executed) with five additional discipline items absorbed from WO-0058 → WO-0062 (Phase 2 Week 8 cohort) and an explicit DEC-0016 cross-reference confirming F06 is NOT blocked by the `feat/WO-0053-lancedb-per-branch` orphan-branch state.
plan_summary: Add `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g2_search.rs` (NEW module) with the UCIL-owned `G2SourceProvider` async trait (per DEC-0008 §4) plus three impls — `RipgrepProvider` (reuses `crate::text_search::text_search` per DEC-0009), `ProbeProvider` (drives the stdio MCP plugin manifest at `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml` via a NEW `PluginManager::run_tools_call` extension per DEC-0015 D2), and `LancedbProvider` (filesystem-existence check on `StorageLayout::branch_vectors_dir(...)/code_chunks.lance/` — `StorageLayout` lives in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs:230` ON MAIN per DEC-0016 cross-ref; the `BranchManager` orphan branch is irrelevant to F06; returns empty `G2SourceResults` until P2-W8-F04 populates the index per DEC-0015 D3) — and a `G2SourceFactory` whose `build()` returns three boxed providers in `[Probe, Ripgrep, Lancedb]` order. Extend `McpServer` with a NEW optional `g2_sources: Option<Arc<G2SourceFactory>>` field and a `with_g2_sources` builder. Modify `handle_search_code` to ADDITIVELY append `_meta.g2_fused` (`serde_json::to_value(&G2FusedOutcome)` projected from `fuse_g2_rrf`) when the factory is set; the legacy `_meta.{tool, source, count, results, symbol_match_count, text_match_count, query, root}` fields stay byte-identical so the frozen `test_search_code_basic` (P1-W5-F09 / WO-0035) continues to pass verbatim. Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_search_code_fused()` at MODULE ROOT of `server.rs` (DEC-0007) using local `TestG2SourceProvider` impls to inject canned per-source `G2SourceResults`. Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F06.sh`. Carry the cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline now at 14 consecutive WOs cleared (WO-0042 → WO-0060) by re-exporting EVERY new public symbol from `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` in this same WO. Apply the W8-cohort discipline carried in `scope_in[16..20]`: pre-flight ≤70-char commit-subject validation per WO-0059 lessons line 607, generic ban-words pre-flight on `mock|fake|stub|fixture` per WO-0061 lessons line 696, upstream-API research checklist per WO-0060 lessons line 644, RFR `Verifier-universal gates` section per WO-0059 lessons line 615, and DEC-0016 cross-reference baked into the LancedbProvider rustdoc.
scope_in: Create `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g2_search.rs` (NEW file). Module-level `//!` doc cites master-plan §5.2 lines 447-461 (G2 fan-out + RRF formula), §15.2 lines 1515-1518 (span hierarchy `ucil.group.search` parent + `ucil.tool.<engine>.search` / `vector_search` children), DEC-0009 (in-process ripgrep), DEC-0015 (this WO's three architectural decisions), DEC-0016 (orphan-branch cross-reference: F06 uses `StorageLayout::branch_vectors_dir` which lives on main; the `BranchManager` orphan branch is irrelevant), and WO-0056 (the canonical RRF math via `ucil_core::fusion::fuse_g2_rrf`). Define `pub trait G2SourceProvider: Send + Sync { fn source(&self) -> ucil_core::G2Source; async fn execute(&self, query: &str, root: &Path, max_results: usize) -> Result<ucil_core::G2SourceResults, G2SearchError>; }` with `#[async_trait::async_trait]` (the `async-trait` dep is already in `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` per WO-0047 / WO-0048 — verify with `cargo tree -p ucil-daemon | rg async-trait`). Define `pub enum G2SearchError` (use `thiserror::Error` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`) carrying variants `Ripgrep(crate::text_search::TextSearchError)`, `Probe(crate::plugin_manager::PluginError)`, `Lancedb(std::io::Error)`, `Timeout`. Each variant ships a substantive `#[error(...)]` message naming the source.

Add three `G2SourceProvider` impls inside `g2_search.rs`. Each impl is `Send + Sync` and exposes a `pub fn new(...) -> Self` constructor:  
  (a) `pub struct RipgrepProvider { /* no fields — pure stateless wrapper around the in-process search */ }`. `source() -> ucil_core::G2Source::Ripgrep`. `execute(&self, query, root, max_results)` calls `crate::text_search::text_search(root, query, max_results)` (already on main per DEC-0009 / WO-0035), then maps each `TextSearchHit` to a `ucil_core::G2Hit { file_path, start_line, end_line: start_line, snippet: <line content> }`. Rank assignment: `hits[0]` is rank 1, `hits[1]` is rank 2, etc. — preserve the order returned by `text_search` so `fuse_g2_rrf`'s rank input is monotone. On `TextSearchError::BuildMatcher`, return `Err(G2SearchError::Ripgrep(...))`. On any other text-search error, also wrap as `Ripgrep`. Wrap the whole body in `tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.tool.ripgrep.search", level = "debug", skip(self), fields(query, max_results))` per master-plan §15.2 line 1517.  
  (b) `pub struct ProbeProvider { manifest_path: PathBuf, /* the plugin manifest path; spawn-per-call shape per DEC-0015 D2 */ }`. `source() -> ucil_core::G2Source::Probe`. `execute(&self, query, root, max_results)` parses the manifest via `crate::plugin_manager::PluginManifest::from_path(&self.manifest_path)`, builds an `arguments` JSON `serde_json::json!({"query": query, "path": root.to_string_lossy(), "maxResults": max_results})` (Probe's `search_code` tool parameter shape — confirm against `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml` and `npx -y @probelabs/probe@0.6.0-rc315 mcp` `tools/list` output during executor research; if Probe's actual `search_code` parameter names differ, update the JSON keys to match — DO NOT silently fail), then calls `crate::plugin_manager::PluginManager::run_tools_call(&manifest, "search_code", &arguments, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS).await` (the NEW PluginManager extension landed in scope_in step 4). Decode the response (`Value` containing `result.content[*].text` or `result.matches[*]` — Probe's actual schema; executor-time research required) into a `Vec<G2Hit>` keyed at `(file_path, start_line, end_line)` with `snippet` populated from the response's snippet field. On `PluginError`, return `Err(G2SearchError::Probe(e))`. Span: `tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.tool.probe.search", level = "debug", skip(self), fields(query, max_results))`. Constructor: `pub fn new(manifest_path: PathBuf) -> Self`.  
  (c) `pub struct LancedbProvider { vectors_dir: PathBuf }`. `source() -> ucil_core::G2Source::Lancedb`. `execute(&self, query, root, max_results)` performs a real filesystem-existence check per DEC-0015 D3: `let table_dir = self.vectors_dir.join("code_chunks.lance");` then `tokio::fs::try_exists(&table_dir).await.map_err(G2SearchError::Lancedb)?`. If the table directory does not exist (i.e. `try_exists` returns `Ok(false)`) OR the directory exists but contains zero entries (read via `tokio::fs::read_dir`, count entries — empty == zero rows in the LanceDB sense), return `Ok(ucil_core::G2SourceResults { source: G2Source::Lancedb, hits: vec![] })` after a `tracing::debug!("LancedbProvider: no embeddings yet at {:?}; returning empty hits per DEC-0015 D3", table_dir);` line. This is REAL filesystem code, not a `todo!()` / `unimplemented!()` / hard-coded `Default::default()` — the function literally does the work and finds no rows. The forward-compat path (when P2-W8-F04 populates the table) is documented in the impl's rustdoc with `# Forward-compat` heading naming the augmentation point: "when P2-W8-F04 lands the indexing pipeline, this body is augmented to open `lancedb::connect(&self.vectors_dir)` and run the actual vector query; the trait signature is unchanged so the call site at `handle_search_code` does not need updating". The rustdoc ALSO carries a `# DEC-0016 cross-reference` heading documenting that `vectors_dir` is constructed from `StorageLayout::branch_vectors_dir(...)` (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs:230` on main) and is NOT routed through the orphan `BranchManager` (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` does not exist on main; LancedbProvider does not import or reference it). Span: `tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.tool.lancedb.vector_search", level = "debug", skip(self), fields(query, max_results))`. Constructor: `pub fn new(vectors_dir: PathBuf) -> Self`. NO new dependency on the `lancedb` crate is added in this WO — the filesystem-only check uses `tokio::fs` which is already a transitive dep.

Add `pub struct G2SourceFactory` to `g2_search.rs`. Holds `probe_manifest_path: PathBuf` + `vectors_dir: PathBuf` and exposes `pub fn new(probe_manifest_path: PathBuf, vectors_dir: PathBuf) -> Self` plus `pub fn build(&self) -> Vec<Box<dyn G2SourceProvider + Send + Sync + 'static>>` returning the three impls in `[Box::new(ProbeProvider), Box::new(RipgrepProvider), Box::new(LancedbProvider)]` order. The factory is `Clone` (cheap — clones two `PathBuf`s). Per WO-0049 lessons line 12 / WO-0049 scope_in step 2 the factory pattern keeps `McpServer::Clone` working without consuming the providers by-move on every request.

Extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` with a NEW public async method `pub async fn run_tools_call(manifest: &PluginManifest, tool_name: &str, arguments: &serde_json::Value, timeout_ms: u64) -> Result<serde_json::Value, PluginError>` per DEC-0015 D2. The method:  
  (a) Spawns the plugin via the same `Self::spawn(manifest)?` path that `health_check_with_timeout` uses (`plugin_manager.rs:1048-1086`).  
  (b) Drives the MCP protocol prefix `initialize` → `notifications/initialized` (factor out a NEW private helper `async fn run_protocol_prefix(child: &mut Child) -> Result<(), PluginError>` shared with the existing `run_tools_list` to avoid duplicating the protocol prefix code per DEC-0015 D2 rationale paragraph; `run_tools_list` and `run_tools_call` both call it).  
  (c) Sends `tools/call` with `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":<tool_name>,"arguments":<arguments>}}\n`.  
  (d) Reads ONE response frame; parses it as JSON; returns `response.result` (the unparsed `Value` so the caller knows the tool's schema).  
  (e) On a JSON-RPC error envelope (`response.error.code` present), surfaces as `PluginError::ProtocolError(format!("tools/call returned JSON-RPC error: code={}, message={}", code, message))` — matches `run_tools_list`'s contract for malformed responses.  
  (f) The whole call is wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms), ...)` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` async-IO timeout rule. On `Elapsed`, returns `PluginError::Timeout`.  
  Add full rustdoc citing DEC-0015 D2 + the three callers (F06 Probe, future P2-W8-F08 `find_similar`, future Phase-3 host adapters per DEC-0015 D2 rationale).  
  The existing `health_check_with_timeout`'s call site `Self::run_tools_list(&mut child).await` becomes `Self::run_protocol_prefix(&mut child).await?; Self::send_tools_list(&mut child).await` (where `send_tools_list` is the renamed half of the old `run_tools_list` that ONLY sends `tools/list` and parses the reply — the rename keeps the old behaviour byte-identical for `health_check`).

Extend `pub struct McpServer` (in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`) with a NEW field `pub g2_sources: Option<Arc<g2_search::G2SourceFactory>>`. Add a builder method `pub fn with_g2_sources(mut self, factory: Arc<g2_search::G2SourceFactory>) -> Self { self.g2_sources = Some(factory); self }`. The existing `McpServer::new()` and `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg)` constructors MUST stay byte-identical in behaviour — the `g2_sources` field defaults to `None`, and the existing `handle_search_code` path falls through to the legacy P1-W5-F09 KG+ripgrep merge when the factory is absent. This preserves WO-0010's `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` and the frozen `server::test_search_code_basic` (P1-W5-F09).

Modify `handle_search_code` (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:747-795`) to ADDITIVELY emit `_meta.g2_fused` per DEC-0015 D1. Existing logic — argument parsing, KG `read_symbol_matches`, in-process `text_search::text_search`, and the legacy `merge_search_results` + `search_code_response` envelope — STAYS byte-identical. After the legacy envelope is constructed, IF `self.g2_sources.is_some()`: use `if let Some(factory) = self.g2_sources.as_ref()` to avoid `.expect()` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`; call `let providers = factory.build();` (3 boxed impls); fan out via `let outputs = futures::future::join_all(providers.into_iter().map(|p| async move { p.execute(&query, &root, max_results).await }));` with each provider wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(G2_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE, ...)` (NEW `pub const G2_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_secs(2);` defined alongside `G2_MASTER_DEADLINE = Duration::from_secs(5);` at server.rs module-root). Collect the per-source `G2SourceResults` into a `Vec<G2SourceResults>` (silently dropping `Err` and `Timeout` — partial-results semantics matching `fuse_g1`'s behaviour from WO-0048). Pass the vec to `ucil_core::fuse_g2_rrf(&per_source)` and serialize the resulting `G2FusedOutcome` via `serde_json::to_value(&fused).expect("G2FusedOutcome serializes")`. Inject the resulting `Value` under `_meta.g2_fused` of the legacy envelope: `env["result"]["_meta"]["g2_fused"] = fused_value;`. The handler now needs to be `async` because `provider.execute(...)` is async — change `fn handle_search_code(...)` to `async fn handle_search_code(...)` and the dispatch site (server.rs:537-541) to `return Self::handle_search_code(id, params, kg, self.g2_sources.as_ref()).await;`. Wrap the parent fan-out in `tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.search", level = "debug", skip_all)` per master-plan §15.2 line 1515.

Add `mod g2_search;` and `pub use g2_search::{G2SearchError, G2SourceFactory, G2SourceProvider, LancedbProvider, ProbeProvider, RipgrepProvider};` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`. Maintain alphabetical-within-block ordering (the existing `pub use g1_sources::{...}` block landed in WO-0049 if that branch were on main; here we ONLY add the g2 block — see scope_out below). The lib.rs preamble paragraph for WO-0048 / WO-0056 gets a NEW trailing sentence appended: 'WO-0063 for `P2-W7-F06` lights up [`server::McpServer::with_g2_sources`] and the G2-fused half of the `search_code` MCP tool (master-plan §3.2 row 4 / §5.2 G2 fan-out): the handler fans out via [`g2_search::G2SourceFactory::build`], runs the three providers in parallel ([`g2_search::ProbeProvider`] / [`g2_search::RipgrepProvider`] / [`g2_search::LancedbProvider`]), and fuses via `ucil_core::fuse_g2_rrf` (WO-0056). The legacy P1-W5-F09 KG+ripgrep merge stays byte-identical per DEC-0015 D1; the fused output appears on a new additive `_meta.g2_fused` field. [`g2_search::LancedbProvider`] returns empty `hits` until P2-W8-F04 populates the per-branch `code_chunks.lance` table per DEC-0015 D3.' Same deferral-signpost discipline as WO-0045 / WO-0047 / WO-0048 / WO-0049.

Add the frozen acceptance test `pub async fn test_search_code_fused()` annotated `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The selector matches `server::test_search_code_fused` per `feature-list.json:P2-W7-F06.acceptance_tests[0].selector`. Test setup uses the local `TestG2SourceProvider` shape per DEC-0008 §4 (UCIL-owned trait, local impls in tests are NOT critical-dep mocks per WO-0048 lessons line 363):  
  (a) Define a private `struct TestG2SourceProvider { source: ucil_core::G2Source, hits: Vec<ucil_core::G2Hit> }` inside `#[cfg(test)] mod search_code_fused_support { ... }` (or as `mod`-root `#[cfg(test)]` items adjacent to the test). Implement `G2SourceProvider` for it returning a clone of the canned `hits` regardless of query.  
  (b) Define a `TestG2SourceFactory` that holds three `TestG2SourceProvider`s and exposes `build()` to return them; OR pass the three providers via a NEW test-only constructor on `G2SourceFactory` (e.g. `pub(crate) fn from_providers(...)` gated on `#[cfg(test)]`).  
  (c) Per-source canned hits cover overlapping + distinct locations:  
    - Probe: `[(util.rs, 10, 20, snippet="fn foo() // probe"), (util.rs, 30, 40, snippet="fn baz() // probe")]`  
    - Ripgrep: `[(util.rs, 10, 20, snippet="fn foo() // ripgrep"), (other.rs, 5, 5, snippet="// other ripgrep")]`  
    - Lancedb: `[]` (per DEC-0015 D3 default-empty path; the test exercises the canonical Phase-2 main path)  
  (d) Build a `KnowledgeGraph` via `KnowledgeGraph::open_in_memory()` (the existing test helper used by `test_search_code_basic` — confirm the constructor name during executor research) seeded with one `Entity` so the legacy KG+ripgrep merge produces a non-empty `_meta.results` array — the test verifies the legacy fields are preserved AND the new `_meta.g2_fused` is populated.  
  (e) Construct `let server = McpServer::new().with_knowledge_graph(kg).with_g2_sources(factory);`.  
  (f) Drive a `tools/call` for `search_code` with `arguments.query = "foo"`, `arguments.root = <path-to-fixture-or-tempdir>`, `arguments.max_results = 50`. Use the same `tokio::io::duplex` + `BufReader` pattern as `test_all_22_tools_registered` (server.rs:1452-1530).  
  (g) **Sub-assertion 1** — JSON-RPC envelope is well-formed: `jsonrpc == "2.0"`, `id` echoes the request, no `error` field, `result.isError == false`.  
  (h) **Sub-assertion 2 — legacy fields preserved**: `result._meta.tool == "search_code"`, `result._meta.source == "tree-sitter+ripgrep"`, `result._meta.symbol_match_count` is present, `result._meta.text_match_count` is present, `result._meta.results` is a JSON array — the WO-0035 / P1-W5-F09 shape from `test_search_code_basic`. (Per DEC-0015 D1 — additive evolution.)  
  (i) **Sub-assertion 3 — g2_fused present**: `result._meta.g2_fused.hits` is a JSON array. Length is the number of fused hits (3 — `(util.rs, 10, 20)` Probe+Ripgrep merged, `(util.rs, 30, 40)` Probe-only, `(other.rs, 5, 5)` Ripgrep-only).  
  (j) **Sub-assertion 4 — RRF ranking applied**: `result._meta.g2_fused.hits[0].file_path == "util.rs"` AND `start_line == 10` AND `contributing_sources` is `["Probe", "Ripgrep"]` (descending `rrf_weight` per WO-0056 line 286-296). The merged hit has the highest fused_score because Probe×2.0 + Ripgrep×1.5 at the same location beats Probe-only at `(util.rs, 30, 40)` (only Probe×2.0) and Ripgrep-only at `(other.rs, 5, 5)` (only Ripgrep×1.5).  
  (k) **Sub-assertion 5 — snippet selection**: the top hit's `snippet == "fn foo() // probe"` (Probe wins on weight). This proves the handler is calling `fuse_g2_rrf`'s snippet-selection logic, not a custom dedup. (Per WO-0056 line 525 — the snippet of the highest-rrf_weight contributing source wins.)  
  (l) **Sub-assertion 6 — per_source_ranks captured**: `result._meta.g2_fused.hits[0].per_source_ranks` includes both `["Probe", 1]` and `["Ripgrep", 1]` — proves the handler is preserving WO-0056's `per_source_ranks` provenance.  
  Each assertion uses `assert_eq!` / `assert!` with operator-actionable panic messages quoting the actual JSON content on failure (WO-0051 lessons line 405 / WO-0055 lessons line 440 standing pattern).

Add `pub async fn test_search_code_fused_no_factory()` adjacent module-root test covering the legacy-path-only behaviour: `McpServer::new().with_knowledge_graph(kg)` (no `with_g2_sources`) drives a `search_code` request and asserts `result._meta.g2_fused` is ABSENT (`result._meta.get("g2_fused").is_none()`) while every legacy `_meta` field is present. This proves the `Option<Arc<G2SourceFactory>>` slot's `None` path is the byte-identical legacy behaviour per DEC-0015 D1.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F06.sh` per the standing convention. Script must: (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_search_code_fused -- --nocapture`, (b) tee output through the WO-0042 alternation regex `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'`, (c) emit `[FAIL] P2-W7-F06: <reason>` on non-zero exit, (d) include a smoke-call against the legacy regression `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_search_code_basic -- --nocapture` and the no-factory variant `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_search_code_fused_no_factory -- --nocapture` and (e) include a `shellcheck` PATH-fallback per WO-0044 standing pattern (`if command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then shellcheck "$0"; else echo "[INFO] shellcheck not on PATH; skipping shellcheck step."; fi`). Make executable: `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W7-F06.sh`.

**Compose with `fuse_g2_rrf` WITHOUT modifying its body.** WO-0056 froze `fuse_g2_rrf`'s signature and behaviour. F06 ONLY consumes the function from `ucil_core::fusion` via `pub use` re-exports — does NOT touch `crates/ucil-core/**`. The 8 WO-0056 public symbols (`fuse_g2_rrf`, `rrf_weight`, `G2FusedHit`, `G2FusedOutcome`, `G2Hit`, `G2Source`, `G2SourceResults`, `G2_RRF_K`) all stay byte-identical.

**No new top-level `Cargo.toml` workspace dep.** The filesystem-only `LancedbProvider` uses `tokio::fs`; the `ProbeProvider` uses the existing `serde_json` + `tokio::process::Command` (already in `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`); the `RipgrepProvider` uses `crate::text_search` (already on main). NO `lancedb` crate dep is added in this WO — `LancedbProvider` is filesystem-only per DEC-0015 D3. NO `futures` dep addition — if `futures::future::join_all` isn't already a dep, use `tokio::join!` (3-arm) or a manual `[provider.execute().await, provider.execute().await, provider.execute().await]` collect; the executor picks the existing-dep path during research.

**`PluginManager::run_tools_call` is a NEW public method.** Internally factor out `run_protocol_prefix` (NEW private helper) shared with the existing `run_tools_list`; the existing `health_check_with_timeout`'s end-to-end behaviour (initialize → notifications/initialized → tools/list → parse) MUST remain byte-identical. Verifier confirms via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manifests::probe_manifest_health_check` (and `ast_grep_manifest_health_check`) staying green.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 / WO-0049 scope_in step 14 — `let _ = arg;` + early `return Default::default()` — when the literal sed produces a compile-failure cascade. Document BOTH the literal sed AND the runtime-only variant for every pre-baked mutation in the `acceptance` block below.

Optional but recommended: add a brief `# Examples` rustdoc block on `handle_search_code` showing a 3-source fan-out case (Probe + Ripgrep contributing to the same location → bucketed into the highest-fused-score `g2_fused.hits[0]`). Backticks every uppercase identifier per WO-0043 lessons line 128 — pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/g2_search.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` returns ZERO new hits relative to `git show main:<file> | rg -cE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b'` (per AC02).

**[WO-0063 ADDITIVE — W8-cohort discipline #1] Pre-flight ≤70-char commit-subject validation.** Per WO-0059 lessons line 607 / WO-0061 lesson on AC24 commit-subject overshoot: BEFORE pushing each commit, run `git log -1 --pretty=%s | awk '{print length}'` on the staged commit subject. If the value > 70, abort the commit and re-craft a shorter subject. Post-push `--amend` is forbidden by root CLAUDE.md, so an oversize subject becomes structurally unfixable. Suggested 70-char-max subjects for the 4 prescribed commits: (1) `feat(daemon): add G2SourceProvider trait + 3 impls (g2_search.rs)` (60 chars); (2) `feat(daemon): factor run_protocol_prefix; add run_tools_call` (58 chars); (3) `feat(daemon): wire McpServer.with_g2_sources + g2_fused emit` (60 chars); (4) `test(daemon): add test_search_code_fused + verify script` (56 chars). Pre-flight grep before each `git commit`: `git diff --cached --stat | head -1`.

**[WO-0063 ADDITIVE — W8-cohort discipline #2] Generic ban-words pre-flight.** Per WO-0061 lessons line 696 (3 consecutive WOs ate post-push docs-cleanup commits for benign rustdoc prose containing `mock|fake|stub|fixture`): BEFORE each push, run `rg -ni 'mock|fake|stub|fixture' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g2_search.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs scripts/verify/P2-W7-F06.sh` and verify any matches are EITHER (a) the literal `TestG2SourceProvider` test-impl name (allowed — it's a test type), OR (b) inside a `#[cfg(test)]` block (allowed — test-only code), OR (c) inside a docstring marked `// ALLOW-MOCK-WORD: <reason>` immediately above the offending line (explicit allowlist). Any other match means the executor must rephrase. Acceptable substitutes: `mock` → `local-impl` / `test-impl` / `injected provider`, `fixture` → `seeded data` / `canned input` / `test setup`. The verify script's prose MUST also avoid these words unless explicitly marked.

**[WO-0063 ADDITIVE — W8-cohort discipline #3] Upstream-API research checklist for first-time consumers.** Per WO-0060 lessons line 644 — F06 first-time-consumes (a) `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml`'s actual MCP `tools/call` argument schema (not just the manifest TOML), and (b) the WO-0044 `plugin_manifests.rs` `FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000` constant. BEFORE writing `ProbeProvider::execute`, the executor MUST: (i) run `npx -y @probelabs/probe@0.6.0-rc315 mcp` once and capture `tools/list` output (the MCP `params.tools[].inputSchema` for `search_code`) — record the actual parameter names (`query`? `pattern`? `path`? `cwd`? `maxResults`? `max_results`? `limit`?) in the RFR; (ii) run `npx -y @probelabs/probe@0.6.0-rc315 mcp` `tools/call` for `search_code` with sample args and record the response envelope shape (`result.content[].text` vs `result.matches[]` vs other) in the RFR. If Probe's actual schema differs from the assumed `{"query", "path", "maxResults"}` shape in scope_in step 2(b), update the JSON keys to match — DO NOT silently fail or pretend. Document the actual schema in `g2_search.rs:ProbeProvider::execute`'s rustdoc.

**[WO-0063 ADDITIVE — W8-cohort discipline #4] RFR `Verifier-universal gates` section.** Per WO-0059 lessons line 615: the ready-for-review marker `ucil-build/work-orders/0063-ready-for-review.md` MUST contain a top-level `## Verifier-universal gates` section explicitly enumerating which AUTHORITY-side gates the verifier will run BEYOND this WO's explicit acceptance_criteria — namely (a) `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` (AC22 — already pinned in this WO, so doubly-explicit), (b) `bash scripts/verify/P2-W7-F06.sh` (already pinned), (c) `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` (AC21 — workspace-wide regression), (d) the verifier-protocol's `cargo clean && cargo test` clean-slate rerun per root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract. Pre-listing them in the RFR prevents the WO-0059-pathology of an implicit gate independently rejecting a green WO.

**[WO-0063 ADDITIVE — W8-cohort discipline #5] DEC-0016 cross-reference baked into the LancedbProvider rustdoc.** Already specified in scope_in step 2(c)'s `# DEC-0016 cross-reference` heading. ALSO: at the top of `g2_search.rs` module-level `//!` doc, add a sentence: 'This module does NOT depend on `crate::branch_manager` (which lives only on the orphan branch `feat/WO-0053-lancedb-per-branch` per DEC-0016) — `LancedbProvider` constructs its `vectors_dir` field from `StorageLayout::branch_vectors_dir` (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs:230` on main).
